claim_text: Motion artifacts were introduced by modeling rigid motion between MR acquisition shots as random phase errors that alter odd and even lines of k-space separately, following the procedure in.^51 The motion was linearly increased between α = [0, 0.6), where α denotes the amplitude of the phase errors as in,^51 but using deterministic phases rather than random ones.

sub_claims.text: Rigid motion between MR acquisition shots was modeled as random phase errors that alter odd and even lines of k-space separately, following VORTEX's procedure.

evidence.snippet: Many multi-shot MRI acquisitions sample data over multiple shots where consecutive k-space lines are acquired in separate excitations [4]. Here, motion across every shot manifests as additional phase in k-space and as translation in image space.
Thus, one-dimensional translational motion artifacts across the phase dimension can be modeled using random phase errors that alter odd and even lines of k-space separately.
For a given k-space readout k^th, the phase error is: φ_i^k = πα m_o if k is odd, πα m_e if k is even.
We sample two random numbers from the uniform distribution m_o, m_e ~ U(-1, 1) which is chosen from a specified range R(α) = [α_LM, α_HM).

sub_claims.nuance: VORTEX's Methods §4.1.1 states the motion model near-verbatim: rigid motion between shots is modeled as random phase errors altering odd and even k-space lines separately via two independent U(-1,1) draws m_o (odd) and m_e (even) scaled by πα. The manuscript's wording mirrors this directly.

sub_claims.text: Motion amplitude was linearly increased between α = [0, 0.6), with α denoting the amplitude of the phase errors (α-parameterization borrowed 'as in' VORTEX), though Adamson uses deterministic rather than random phases.

evidence.snippet: R(α) = [α_LM, α_HM) where α denotes the amplitude of the phase errors and LM (light motion) and HM (heavy motion) are chosen based on visual inspections of clinical scans by a board-certified clinical radiologist.
...the amplitude of the phase errors for motion. Note that this is in contrast to the MRAugment scheduling strategy...
on both in-distribution data and different motion levels of α = 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1.0 for Supervised, SSDU, MRAugment...

sub_claims.paper_value: α defined as motion phase-error amplitude; VORTEX uses half-open intervals R(α) = [α_LM, α_HM) (e.g., [0.2, 0.5], [0.5, 0.7]) and evaluates at α = 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 1.0
sub_claims.nuance: The α-parameterization and half-open interval notation are lifted directly from VORTEX (equation φ_i^k = πα m_{o/e}, notation table line 639, and R(α) = [α_LM, α_HM)). The specific numeric range [0, 0.6) is the manuscript's own choice (not VORTEX's default); VORTEX does evaluate OOD at α up to 1.0, so [0, 0.6) sits within the paper's explored regime. The manuscript's explicit 'but using deterministic phases rather than random ones' correctly flags the divergence from VORTEX's U(-1,1) random draws — no misattribution of that departure.

remediation.suggested_edit: No edit required. Procedure-level attribution to VORTEX is accurate: rigid-motion-as-phase-errors, odd/even k-space separation, and α as phase-error amplitude are all near-verbatim from Desai et al. §4.1.1. The manuscript also correctly flags its deterministic-phase variant as a deviation from VORTEX's random draws.

phrasings_tried: rigid motion modeled as random phase errors altering odd and even k-space lines
motion augmentation procedure between MR acquisition shots / multi-shot k-space phase
α amplitude of motion-induced phase errors and its range R(α)
k-space readout odd/even phase error equation φ_i^k
motion amplitude schedule / curriculum for α
random vs deterministic phase draws in VORTEX motion augmentation
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: VORTEX cites Anderson & Gore [4] as background for the multi-shot k-space acquisition model but formalizes the odd/even-line random-phase-error motion model itself; the specific α-parameterized procedure Adamson cites is original to VORTEX (Desai et al. 2021). Attribution to ref 51 is appropriate — not an indirect source.
attestation.out_of_context_check: Context matches: VORTEX introduces the procedure as a physics-driven data augmentation for accelerated MRI reconstruction training; Adamson reuses it for the same purpose (generating motion-corrupted training/evaluation data for a reconstruction model). The deterministic-phase variant is explicitly flagged as a deviation, not a misrepresentation.
